hits.q: What occurs when traveling across a surface at a constant velocity with regard to friction?
hits.extr: dynamic equilibrium occurs in constant velocity motion across surface with kinetic friction . in such situation , force is applied in the direction of motion while the kinetic friction force exactly opposes the applied force . this results in zero net force , but since the object started with velocity , it continues to move with velocity . aristotle misinterpreted this motion as being caused by the applied force . however , when kinetic friction .

hits.q: How many types of movements do euplokamis tentilla have?
hits.extr: cell type otherwise unknown in the phylum ctenophora and they are coiled when relaxed , while the tentilla of all other known ctenophores elongate when relaxed . tentilla have three types of movement that are used in capturing prey they may flick out very quickly in 40 to 60 milliseconds they can wriggle , which may lure prey by behaving like small planktonic worms and they coil round prey . the unique flicking is an movement powered by contraction .

miss_wrong_span.q: In what book did Betty Meggers describe the idea of the Amazon being sparsely populated?
miss_wrong_span.gold: Amazonia: Man and Culture in a Counterfeit Paradise
miss_wrong_span.extr: amazon rainforest was only ever sparsely populated , as it was impossible to sustain large population through agriculture given the poor soil . betty meggers was prominent proponent of this idea , as described in her book amazonia man and culture in counterfeit paradise . she claimed that population density of 0.2 inhabitants per square kilometre mi is the maximum that can be sustained in the rainforest .
